summary: Drew DeVault shares his September 2021 progress across several FOSS projects. He announces visurf, a minimal Wayland-only frontend for the NetSurf browser with vi-inspired keybindings. The Hare programming language has seen significant progress following a codegen rewrite, including language design refinements and new features. SourceHut has gained GraphQL API improvements and new platform support.
key_points: Visurf is a new minimal, Wayland-only NetSurf browser frontend with vi keybindings, already gaining features like touch support and tabs
The Hare programming language completed a codegen rewrite enabling long-pending language design refactoring, with work on match expressions, tagged unions, and reflection ahead
SourceHut's lists.sr.ht gained a writable GraphQL API and the first implementation of GraphQL-native webhooks
The update reflects DeVault's consistent focus on building minimal, principled alternatives to mainstream tools
money_quotes: I am building a minimal Wayland-only frontend for the NetSurf web browser which uses vi-inspired keybindings.
Following the codegen rewrite we have completed some long-pending refactoring to parts of the language design.
We are also looking into some experiments with reflection.
lists.sr.ht now has a writable GraphQL API, along with the first implementation of GraphQL-native webhooks.
Please help! It's pretty easy to get involved: join the IRC channel at #netsurf on libera.chat and ask for something to do.
key_insight: DeVault's simultaneous work on a minimal browser, a systems programming language, and a forge platform reflects his vision of rebuilding the software stack from principled foundations.
